title: India's Cockroach party leads thousands of youth in protest to parliament

Demonstrators in the Indian capital have marched to parliament as they demand the sacking of the country's education minister.
 
The youth-led Cockroach movement, started as a satirical youth platform, but has morphed into something larger.
 
Protesters are more galvanised after a hunger-striking social activist was forcibly moved to hospital, against his will.

description: The final stages of the World Cup, with Les Bleus’ elimination and England finishing third; extreme heat and wildfires around the world; and the Tour de France brought to a standstill by the heatwave in France. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

title: Europe: Extreme weather fuels climate concerns
description: Europe is the world's fastest-warming continent, and this summer has already brought record-breaking temperatures, devastating wildfires, hailstorms and tornadoes. While climate change is not responsible for every extreme weather event, scientists say it is making such events more frequent and more intense. As the impacts continue to grow, the outlook for the future is increasingly concerning. Julitte Brown reports.

title: Spain or Argentina, who will win the FIFA World Cup? Ex-player predictions
description: Former player predictions as the European and South American champions clash in a World Cup final for the first time.

title: A Modest Proposal for the Strait Dispute: A Shared Pact Among Gulf States
description: A London-based research group has invoked a now-defunct European pact on coal and steel to suggest forming a coalition of countries that would share control of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
description: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Edge Closer to Wider War After U.S. Service Members Killed
description: The U.S. military said it had targeted Iranian forces that were responsible for an attack on a base in Jordan that left two American service members dead and one missing.

title: Iranians suffer from power blackouts during war and heatwave

title: Summer is smoke season now

title: At least 50 Malian soldiers killed in rebel-jihadist attack

title: The Bayeux tapestry arrives in London for display at British Museum
description: The thousand-year-old, 70-metre-long Bayeux Tapestry, which depicts William the Conqueror's campaign in medieval England, has been carefully removed from its transport crate following its transfer from France. Conservators will begin a detailed condition assessment on Monday before the tapestry is installed in a specially designed display case later in the week.

title: Ukraine: Thousands take to the streets to protest against Fedorov removal
description: Protests continued across Ukraine for a third consecutive day despite the ongoing war, with demonstrators demanding the reinstatement of the popular former defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, following his removal. The demonstrations coincided with two days of meetings between President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the country's top military commanders.

The deaths are the first from hostile fire since a truce with Iran in early April and come after a week of the heaviest fighting since the conflict began in February. title: Trump claims US World Cup a success, despite controversies, ahead of final

But the tournament has not been without its controversies, from hardline US migration policies that excluded fans – and one Somali referee, as well as the president's successful appeal to FIFA to overturn a red card for a US player.
